title: ficus infectoria var. psychotriifolium
intro: Ficus infectoria var. psychotriifolium, also known as Kattha or Mimosa bark, is a plant species that belongs to the fig family (Moraceae). It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ficus infectoria var. psychotriifolium include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of heart disease. The plant can be prepared as a herbal decoction or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
